records.ety: [L. evadere, evasum, e out + vadere to go, walk: cf. F. s'vader. See Wade.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Evaded; p. pr. & vb. n.. Evading.]

defs: To get away from by artifice; to avoid by dexterity, subterfuge, address, or ingenuity; to elude; to escape from cleverly; as, to evade a blow, a pursuer, a punishment; to evade the force of an argument.
quotes: The heathen had a method, more truly their own, of evading the Christian miracles.

defs: To escape; to slip away; -- sometimes with from.
quotes: Unarmed they might Have easily, as spirits evaded swift By quick contraction or remove.

defs: To attempt to escape; to practice artifice or sophistry, for the purpose of eluding.
quotes: The ministers of God are not to evade and take refuge any of these . . . ways.
